notes: v1 qualitative test on deliberately-vulnerable sample APKs. Tooling: this environment has no JVM, so runs use androguard 4.1.4 in a venv at mobile-security-review-workspace/venv (python: venv/Scripts/python.exe). OVAA was source-only (no prebuilt APK; building needs full Android SDK/Gradle), so test case 3 substitutes Allsafe, which exercises the same MASVS-PLATFORM surface (exported components, deep links, WebViews, providers) and more. Prompts are phrased the way a real user would speak, including one with no MASVS/OWASP mention (triggering test).

evals.name: diva-bare-trigger-L1
evals.prompt: Can you do a security review of this Android app? I've only got the APK (diva-beta.apk). Let me know what you find and how bad it is.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers despite no mention of MASVS/OWASP. Triage: SHA-256 + signing + build type recorded. Infers L1 profile and states why. Static findings concentrated in STORAGE (insecure SharedPreferences/SQLite, files), CRYPTO (weak/hardcoded), CODE (hardcoded secrets), each traced MASVS->MASWE->MASTG with severity reflecting THIS app. Static-only runtime claims marked 'Needs dynamic verification'. Anti-FP gate applied (public values not flagged as secrets). Markdown report from report-template.md.

evals.name: insecurebank-explicit-masvs-L2-static-only
evals.prompt: Audit InsecureBankv2.apk against OWASP MASVS. It's a banking app so treat it as high-risk, and I don't have a device set up so static only please.
evals.expected_output: Selects L2 profile (high-risk) and justifies it. Honors 'static only, no device': every runtime-dependent item is confidence 'Needs dynamic verification' and NO dynamic results are fabricated. Strong NETWORK coverage (cleartext config, missing pinning, weak TLS/trust), AUTH (token/session handling client-side), STORAGE. MASVS->MASWE->MASTG traceability throughout, MASVS coverage table shows assessed-vs-pass-vs-na.

evals.name: allsafe-platform-exported-focus
evals.prompt: Here's allsafe.apk. I'm mostly worried about whether other apps on the phone can attack it - exported components, deep links, that kind of thing. Can you check?
evals.expected_output: Focuses on MASVS-PLATFORM: enumerates exported components, checks permission protectionLevel AND the actual handler behavior (reachable/exploitable gate), content providers (path traversal/SQLi/grantUriPermissions), deep links/App Links (autoVerify), WebView exposure, mutable PendingIntents. Does NOT flag signature-permission-protected or inert exports (classic FP table applied). Findings traced MASVS-PLATFORM -> MASWE -> MASTG.
